Annotation: This Ieroboam King of Israel died 26. yeares before Ozias: wherby appeareth that Osee prophesied before Isaie: who began neerer the end of Ozias reigne. Isa.6.
Take a woman that hath been a fornicatrixe: and beget children, which wil also be fornicatours.
The issue of Iehu now reigning, solicited the 10. tribes called Israel to idolatrie, which God here sayth he wil reuenge.
Among manie sinners, some are the elected children of God, whom he wil cal to grace & repentance.
Al the faithfull haue one head, our Sauiour Christ.

Ye people of Iuda disdaine not to cal the ten tribes & the Gentils your brethren and sisters. For God wil make them also his people, as appeareth in the Ghospel, more of the ten tribs then of the other Kingdom belieued in Christ: & afterwards more of the Gentils, then of the Iewes.
God wil not withdraw his punishments til the sinners be penitent.
Gods mercie preuenteth by his grace offered to sinners that they may conuert to him if they wil.
The Apostles S. Paul. Rom.9.v.25. and S.Peter. Ep.1. c.2.v.10. expound this place of the conuersion of Gentils to Christ.
Notwithstanding sinners forsake God, yet he offereth them new grace, neuer hating the persons, but their sinnes.

The Iewes not belieuiug in Christ, but expecting his comming, and in the meane time abstaying from seruing of idols, receiue of God temporal poore meanes to liue, not 30. pieces of siluer, and three cores of wheat, that is, belieuiug in the B. Trinitie, and keeping the ten commandments, they might possesse life euerlasting, but halfe so much: neither wheat but barley, til neere the end of the world: when they shal be conuerted to Christ.

Children of Israel import the whole people of the ten, & two tribes, so he speaketh to al til. v. 11. Knowledge of God includeth the keeping of his commandments, for he that saith, he knoweth God, and keepeth not his commandments, is a lier. 1. Ioan. 2.v.4.
Function of Priefts which is properly sacrifice, being taken away, al spiritual offices decay therwith.
The hosts offered for sinnes.
Certaine sinnes more then others doe obscure mans vnderstanding, but spiritual fornication blindeth the hart aboue al other vices.

It was a greater sinne in the Kingdom of Iuda to commit idolatrie, where they had the publike true seruice of God in the temple, then in Israel, where Ieroboam had set vp calues, and forbid the people from going to Ierusalem.
There were no true priefts in the ten tribes (3. Reg. 12.) but he calleth them by the title which they vsurped, falsly pretending to doe the offices of right Priefts.
The captiuitie is heere described not only in bare words but as in fact it shal happen, with tumults of warre, sounding of trumpets, crying, howling &c.
God who punisheth, is alwayes readie to heale sinners, if they wil repent.

S. Paul (1. Cor.15.v.4.) not only teaching Christs Resurrection, but also expresly saying the third day, according to the Scriptures, seemeth to vnderstand this place, where & in no other place, the day is so cleerely expressed.

God punishing al the Iewes by diuiding their Kingdom into two (3.Reg.12.) would haue cured them: but Ieroboam King of the ten tribes drew al his people into grosser sinnes then before, by making a schisme & setting vp idols to be adored for God.
Al the chiefe men of the ten tribes consented to the schisme & idolatrie of their new King Ieroboam.
Became like to pagane idolaters, and impenitent.
The temple also in Ierusalem (though not so soone) shal be destroyed.
But first the ten tribes of Israel for their general idolatrie shal be caried into captiuitie by the Assyrians.
The two tribes seeing the miseries of the other ten, wil not preuent the like by penance, but think to escape by fortifying their cities.
Manie of the Kingdō of Israel by reasō of famine and other distresses wil fly into Ægypt, as both this place and the same words ch 8. v. 13. doe foreshew. S. Ierom & Theodoret. v.6.
Israel did foolishly prophecie al prosperitie to themselues, not inspired by God, but replenished with furie of madnes.
By how much more & greater benefits Israel receiued of God, so much were they more vngrateful.
The house of one of their calues which Ieroboam set vp for their God, otherwise called Bethel.

From the time that the tribe of Dan adored an idol which they tooke from Micheas (Iud. 18.v.14) which the other Israelites reuenged not, they haue very often either committed or suffered idolatrie, which they ought to haue hindred, & therfore shal at last be punished.

Literally this is spoken of the people of Israel called Gods sonne (Exo.4.v.23.) whom he deliuered out of Ægypt, but mystically is verified of Christ called out of Ægypt Mat.2. and is no lesse certainly the true mysticall sense, the Euangelist induced with the Holie Ghost, so interpreting, then is the literel sense of this or anie other place.

God draweth men by sweet inuitations, by great and manie benefits agreable to mans nature and free wil, by his loue and charitie: not as beasts are drawen by feare & force. Gods propertie is to haue mercie and to pardon. Man is prone to reuenge and punish.
To make shew of turning to God, & to trust more in men, is as vayne as to thinke to feed or to gouerne the winde.
The Historie of Iacob & his children written in Genesis & Exodus, which the Prophet here toucheth, sheweth the singular benefits of God towards this people.

When Ieroboam first set vp the calues to be adored, the people had horrour therof, yet consented therto. and shortly after some added the idol of Baal. 3.Reg 16. & of other idols as this place testifieth.

Euils that happen are al of mans owne procurement by his sinnes: wherof God is no way the autour or cause: who of his part doth al for the help of man: for whether he punish or pardon, al is to saue men: so God is only cause of help and of al good, but not of euil as it is euil.

Such imprecations in holy scriptures are sometimes only predictions, as Psal. 68. v. 23. & so here is prophecied what shal happen to the Israelites in Assyria. S. Ierom. sometimes are the zelous desires of Saints conformable to Gods iustice, as Psal. 149. v. 6. 7. 8. 9.
When the Israelites shal conuert to God, as some did when Christ came, and manie wil neere the end of the world, then Christ wil heale them.
No humane wit is able to vnderstand this (& other Prophets) yet the iust shal know so much as is necessarie. S. Ierom in prœm. S. Aug. l. 18. c. 28. ciuit.

Prophecies perteyne not only to those that then liue, when they are vttered, but also to al posteritie, euen to the end of the world, that it may appeare what is fulfilled, and what yet resteth to come in due time.
When Ierusalem was taken, and the Temple destroyed by the Babylonians, the sacrifice necessarily ceased according to this & other Prophecies.
Fasting, praying and other good workes of manie assembling together, are an especiall meanes to appeaze Gods wrath, prouoked by former sinnes.

Prophets doe often speake in such phrase as if they admonished the people what to doe, when indeed they foreshew what they wil doe in their distresses. In the time when God wil suffer afflictio to fal vpon them for their sinnes.

For better mouing the hart to true repentance, God requireth these external works of penance. And where the same are wanting, at least in wil, it is a manifest signe that the hart is not truly penitent. S. Ierom.

Zele is an indignation rising of loue: when one seeth anie person or thing which he loueth contemned or wronged. So God hath zele for his people, when they are vniustly afflicted, more of the malice of their afflicters then for iustice.
That this is a plaine prophecie of the mission of the Holie Ghost performed on Whitsunday, the fiftith day after Christs Resurrection, & the tenth after his Ascension, S. Peter teacheth Act. 2.
S. Ierom & most other expositers vnderstand this chapter of the general Iudgement, though some expound it of the relaxation of the Iewes from captiuitie, and of the punishment of their enemies.

And so Iosaphat is literally vnderstood the place on the east side of Ierusalem, betweene the Temple and mount Oliuet, whence our Sauiour ascended into heauen. Neither is there anie reason why the Iudgement should rather be in an other place, seeing this is expressed by name, signifying, The Iudgement of our Lord.

This duplication of the word peoples importeth an innumerable multitude in that valley of concision (also repeated) to signifie, that there al Gods enemies shal be vtterly damned to eternal destruction, as it were cut in peeces, as fuel to the fire of hel.

As Dauid was called from keeping sheep made a King & a Prophet; so Amos being a shepheard or heards-man was also made a Prophet. Iosephus l. Antiq. 9. c. 1. sayth this earth-quake hapned when King Ozias presumed to offer incense.

Three signifie the multitude of their sinnes: for three is the first number that is called many, or may be called al. And foure signifie excesse in multitude, so that, albeit God doth forgiue a multitude of sinnes, yet at last for so great excesse he hastneth their punishment.

Besides other sinnes of the Moabites, their crueltie in drawing the bones of the King of Idumea out of the graue, as S. Ierom testifieth by tradition; & their King immolating his owne sonne, 4. Reg. 3. exceeded the rest, & therefore were at last more seuerely punished.
The most heynous sinne in Iuda was, that hauing the law to instruct them, they contemned and transgressed it.
Israel much more contemned the same law of God, & committed the sinnes of al nations.
Sonnes committed incest with their fathers wiues; & the fathers with their daughters in law, which most detestable sinnes must needs be seuerly punished.
By Israel is here vnderstood the whole people al the kinred or spring of Iacob, deliuered from Ægypt.
As two doe not wel trauel together, except they agree: so man can not walke with God, vnles he agree with God, keeping his commandments.
Al euil of paine, that is punishment for sinne, is by Gods permission and ordinance, either to bring sinners to repentance, or (if they dye in mortal sinne) the beginning of eternal punishment.
The fayrest and strongest things that wicked men haue shal at last be destroyed.
Rich hard-harted people, who being welthie haue no compassion of the poore.
After manie admonitions giuen in vaine, God suffereth the idolaters to doe al the wickednes they list.
Al these afflictions God sent to the children of Israel for their good, but they murmured and were stil obstinate.
After long captiuitie Christ wil offer himself to the Iewes, and such as receiue him, he wil saue.
When the people neither feele nor feare euil, God foreseeing their calamities, lamenteth the same in their behalfe, therby admonishing them to know their owne danger, and by repentance to preuent it.

If men seeke good & not euil, God wil assist them: but it is here sayd perhaps God wil haue mercie, by reason of the difficultie of mans part, who conuerteth not alwayes perfectly, as he hath freewil to doe by Gods grace assisting him.
In the first yeare of the 40. and beginning of the second they offered sacrifices to God. Leuit.8. & 9. Num.7. But not afterwards. Aug. q.47. in Exod.
It is a foule & odious fault when rich men neglect the poore, but most detestable when the rich in Sion, welthie Clergie men haue not compassion on them that want either spiritual or temporal helpe.

Wheras Iacob by humilitie, patience, & manie other vertues, became great in Gods fauour: now his progenie by pride & delicacie become hatefull to God in respect of these sinnes, & are therfore afflicted, and miserably slaine and caried captiues.

To turne the workes of iudgement & iustice, into sinnes, which are bitter & vngrateful, is as contrarie to order, as it is contrarie to the course of nature, that horses should runne vpon craggie rocks, or wild bufles be brought to draw the plough.
Locusts swarming in multitude signified the Assyrian soldiers inuading the ten tribes, as is recorded 4. Reg.18.
Salmanassar King of Assyrians hauing subdued the ten tribes, inuaded the Kingdō of Iuda, & besieged Ierusalem: but his armie was miraculously destroyed. 4. Reg.19.
This second vision of fire signified the captiuitie of the two tribes caried into Babylon. 4. Reg.14. & 25.
This third vision signified the calamities which the ten tribes suffered shortly after in the reigne of Manahem. 4. Reg.15.v.20.
False Prophets not induring the wholsome preaching of true Pastours falsely accuse them of rebellion, treason, and sedition.

In this also the false Prophet lyed; for Amos sayd not: Ieroboam shal dye by the sword: but that God would rise vpon the house of Ieroboam with the sword. v. 9. fulfilled when Zacharias the sonne of Ieroboam was slaine by Sellum. 4. Reg. 15.v.10.

By this vision of a hook is signified, that not only the neerer parts of the ten tribes should be brought into captiuitie, but also the rest which were further off: as fruit of trees, which cannot be gathered with the hand, is drawen with a hooke.
In their great prosperitie, when they least suspect, calamitie shal fal vpon them.
It can not be doubted but in the siege there was want of bread, drink, & of other victuals: but greater want of spiritual foode.
Destruction of the Altar, and the Temple, importe the abolishing of sacrifice, when the two tribes were carried into Babylon.
God, who defendeth his Church as a strong bundel fast bond together, wil punish the wicked with iust afflictions.
Notwithstanding the great ruine of the Iewes, slaine and led captiues, yet God in them conserued the Church that it was not destroyed.
S. Iames conformably to S. Peters doctrine, interpreteth this place of the conuersion of Gentils to Christ. Act. 15. v. 15.
God directed the cogitations of diuers other Gentiles, to ioyne their forces against the Idumeans.
God admonisheth them what they ought not to doe, but withal foresheweth that they wil despise the Iewes their brethren: & wil reioyce in their miseries.

Historically al this prosperitie was promised to the Iewes after their deliuerie from Babylon, and so much thereof performed as their new sinnes hindred not. the rest is fulfilled in Christians. S. Ierom. ep. ad Dardian.

The rest of this prophecie is only of Christ possessing al nations. To whom al the Prophets giue testimonie that al receiue remission of sinnes by his name belieuing in him. Act. 10. v. 43. See S. Aug. li. 10. c. 31. de ciuit.
God Creatour & Lord of al the world, hath also care of al, Rom. 3. v. 29. & therfore sent this Prophet to the great citie Niniue.
Ionas sheweth the cause why he fled, ch. 4. v. 1. fearing to be counted a false Prophet.
The mariners seeing no natural cause of so sudaine & great a tempest, ought to know the reason therof by lot. the Prophet agreed, by Gods inspiration, & so was discouered.
Sacrifice & vowes are knowne to al men by the light of nature to be grateful to God.
That this great fish was a whale, our Sauiour plainly expresseth. Mat. 11. v. 40.

The Prophet doubtles prayed before & when they cast him out of the ship, and continued the same prayer being in the whales bellie, with more confidence, that he should be safely cast on the drie land.
Furthest that can be from mountaines, euen into the depth of the sea, which is lower then any other valleyes.
Gods wil is his word by which al creatures were made, and to which, as wel liuing, as sensifes things obey.

Diodorus Siculus li. 2.c.1. writeth that Niniue was in length 150 stadia, or forlongs, in breadth 90. so it was in circuite of the wals 480. And euerie forlong hauing 125 paces of fiue foot the compasse was 60. Italian myles, about 50. or 48. English myles: a sufficient trauel of three dayes, to passe through the principal streetes.

As wel this, as many other like prophecies, shew that Gods threats are conditional, it sinners wil repent: for then God changeth his sentence. S.Chrys. S.Iero. S.Greg. The same is also cleere Iere.18.v.8.
Great remorse and detestation of sinne maketh penitents to exceede in austere works, which being wel meant is accepted at Gods hands, so it be not indiscreet.

Ionas conceiued by their penance that God would for this time spare Niniue, & so was afflicted, fearing that both this, & other prophecies should be reputed vncertaine. But this doubt is solued vnderstanding Gods threats to be conditional. For they falling againe into former sinnes were afterwards destroyed. Nahum.1.2.& 3.
If the Prophet should not admonish the people, both he and they should dye in their sinne. Iere.3.v.18. but he discharging his office they not repenting shal perish in their iniquitie.
Samaria gathering riches by traffike with idolaters communicated also with them in idolatrie, and therfore their riches shal be caried into Ninive and other places of Assyria.

Tel not these calamities which I foreshew amongst your enemies, lest they reioyce therat. But lament in your owne houses which shal be ruined. S. Ierome here testifieth the hardnes of this place praying for the assistance of the Holy Ghost to vnderstand it.
Ironiously he sayth the glorie, that is, the miserie of Israel shal be extended to Odolla the vttermost citie in Iuda.
By the figure Lipiate, that is here called vnprofitable, which is indeed extreme hurtful, & hath nothing profitable in it.
The Princes of the people command the Prophets not to speake & inculcate so much of future afflictions.

God answereth, that his threates proceed from his mercie. For he would saue al, and those that either walke rightly or repent shal feele the effect of his mercie, but except they be admonished, neither the good wil perseuere in goodnes, nor the euil returne into the right way.
In further proofe of Gods mercie he promiseth to gather his Church of al nations by Christ.
The chief of both the Kingdoms were great extorcioners, & the poore murmuring against the rich also extorted ech from other: imitating the greater sort in euil.
False Prophets sought also their priuate gaine.
Likewise the Iudges peruerted iudgement, scraping their temporal profite of other mens quarels.

The Iewes confesse this Prophecie to be of Christ the promised Messias: but denie it to be of our Sauiour Iesus of Nazareth. The later dayes are this whole time from Christ to the end of the world, which is the last time, after which shal be no other state of time, but eternitie.
Al such quiet patient people as walke in the name of God, wil keep this peace yea euen with them that hate peace, suffering persecution with alacritie of mind.
The Iewes shal be at last conuerted to Christ.

Jerusalem is called daughter of the spoiler, that is, addicted to spoyling & oppressing the poore: & therfore shal be spoyled; yet shal be restored & conserued, til Bethlehe bring forth the Dominatour, Christ our Lord.
Bethlehem a smal citie, of least account amongst many, yet by Christ borne there, it became not a litle one, but more excellent then many others. Mat. 2. v. 6.

Wheras God shewed most singular great benefits, the vngrateful malignant people requited him with most wicked & reprochful crueltie. As is excellently set forth in the Churches seruice on good friday.
Both Moyses & Aaron were figures of our Sauiour, and their sister Marie a figure of B. Marie the Mother of Christ.
Workes of iustice & mercie doe farre excel external sacrifice of the old law.
Thou Ierusalem art so wicked & foolish, as to imitate the most wicked Kings & people of the ten tribes Amri & Achab. 3. Reg. 16.
Such general speach doth not importe absolutely al without exception, but that very many or the farre greater part of the wicked remained so stil, notwithstanding the Prophets diligence in preaching.
Belieue not a freind, and trust not in Prince: euen domestical may not be trusted, because euerie one in such case hath care of himselfe though it be with preiudice of others.
Babylo shal be taken and spoyled by the Medes & Persians, by them the Iewes shal be deliuered: whereat the Chaldees wil much repine.
Pastours office is to feede and rule with meate, and rod, with doctrine and discipline.
God gaue truth to Iacob, performing that which was promised of his mercie to Abraham. Gen.22. v.17.

Nahum prophecied about 50. yeares after Ionas, neer 136. before the destruction of Niniue. Burden signifieth comminatorie & pensiue prophecie of ruine & punishment. Allegorically Nahum according to his name, comforteth the iust, shewing that God wil reuenge them by destroying Niniue.
The Assirians accounting themselues assured to take & spoile Ierusalem, & therupon banketting and drinking were defeated al in one night.
To Senacherib succeeded his sonne Asarhaddon, but presently after the whole lineage was destroyed. 4.Reg. 19.
Nabuchodonosor inuading the territorie of Niniue wasted al things, & then assaulted and tooke the citie.
The Assirians became more proud & insolent after they had spoiled the two tribes and caried the ten into captiuitie, & therfore God wil now reuenge this pride.
The people of Niniue, when the wal of the citie shal once be broken by the enemies, wil flye away as water runneth out of a pond or fishpoole, when the banke is broke.
The King of Assirians like a furious lion gathered praye out of al cuntries, & brought it into Niniue as into his denne, but at last shal be spoyled of al.

Nemrod began with sheding bloud to make himselfe great. Gen 10. so Ninus who built Niniue, & their successours were stil very bloudie, & otherwise wicked, but at last after 1200. yeares, vnder Sardanapalus their Monarchie decayed much.
This citie was first called No, but being destroyed by the Chaldees, and reedified by K. Alexander, was then called by his name. S. Iero.

This Prophet expresseth not against what Kingdome citie or person this burden is: the reason wherof seemeth to be, because it is against very many and diuers, yea against al persecuters of Gods seruants.
S. Paul alleadgeth this place in the mystical sense Act. 13. in the literal the coherence is very obscure.
The Chaldees were not yet come to their greatnes, and therfore this could not be the same Habacuc mentioned Daniel. 14. v. 32.
After that the Chaldees shal haue subdued the Assyrians, they shal also be ouerthrowne by others, to wit, by the Medes and Persians.
The Chaldees and other victorious nations conquering other countries attribute al to their owne industrie, & forces, honouring themselues, & not God.
The words of the Prophet expecting what God wil further reueale vnto him.
He that commeth at the time apointed, though it be long, is not slacke.

The principal comforte of the iust consisteth in their faith & confidence of the world to come. Wherby they liue with consolation, wheras otherwise this miserable life were rather a death. Heb.10. v.38.
For much bloudshed by the Chaldees, for auarice, iniustice & other wickednes they shal at last be ruined.
Whiles thou thoughtest by rapine & auarice to eternize thy familie and Kingdom thou hast merited the ruine therof.
For the great excellent, & admirable mercie of God, I was astonished as one afrayd, seeing God himselfe wil take mans nature, & therin pay ransom, and redeeme mankind.

in the time disigned for this purpose. The 70. Interpreters reade, between two liuing creatures thou shal be found, & so the Church hath in the office of Christs Natiuitie & Circumcision: between an oxe and an asse in the stal.
From Bethleem which is southward from Ierusalem.

In Madian, a part of Æthiopia, the people liued most in tentes, not in houses: so here is signified that in the tumulte of warres wil be much remouing of skinnes, that is, of their tentes made of skins.
Antichrist the head of the malignant house, or conuenticle, shal be destroyed by Christ. Isa. 11.v.4.2.Thes.2.
Al afflictions are to be patiently sustayned, that we may haue rest in the day of iudgement.
In saying the word of our Lord, the Prophets signifie that they are not the principal authours of that which they preach or writ, but the ministers by whom God speaketh.
Gathering more comonly signifieth a benefite, but by that which foloweth, from the face of the earth, it is manifest, that God here threatneth to destroy sinners, the Kingdom of Iuda.
Whosoeuer ioyneth false Gods with God Almightie, indeed serueth not God.
The day of punishment is commonly called the day of our Lord. Isa.2. Ioel 1. 1.Cor.5.2. Thess.1.

Ioachaz was depriued of his Kingdom and died in Ægypt. 4. Reg.23. Ioakim was continually vexed by the Babylonians & other nations, at last slaine, & his bodie cast out of the citie. Iere. 22. Sedecias taken, his eyes put out, so caried into Babylon, & al his sonnes slaine.
Al these afflictions are neere.
Repeating & inculcating the same termes, doth elegantly describe the greatnes of the future calamities.
Ye that deserue not Gods loue, but rather to be reiected, yet by repentance returne to him and he wil receiue you.

It is very frequent in the Prophets to speake of things to come as if they were done already, for the certaintie therof. These prophecies of the destructions of other nations by the Chaldees, doe confirm that which is threatened to the Iewes, for al sinning, al must be punished.
Niniue exceeding glorious for antiquitie, greatnes, riches, warlike prowesse, & most large dominio, was at last destroyed.

Ierusalem without proper merits preferred by Gods special grace before al other place, sanctified, adorned, protected most singularly, yet stil prouoked him to wrath, contemning his admonitions, & persisting & multiplying sinnes, can not but at last be seuerely punished.

About 40. yeares after Christs resurrection, the most part of the Iewes persisting obstinate were brought to meruelous distresse & miseries, when Titus tooke & destroyed Ierusalem: which is also a figure of the destruction of this world, and of eternal punishment of the wicked.
Al nations shal inuocate one God in a chosen lip or tongue, in vnitie of fayth, & with one shoulder of fortitude, beare the yoke & burden of Christian life, made sweete and light by Christs grace.
Men of light conuersation, & contemners of Christ, shal also be conuerted, become graue, & greatly honour him.

Zorobabel descending directly from the Kings of Iuda was now duke, & chiefe temporal Gouerner of the Iewes, by permission of Darius King of Persians. In like sort Iesus succeeded in the office of high Priest to Iosedec, who was caried with others captiue into Babylon. 1. Par. 6. v. 15.

It behoueth without delay to set forward restauration of Gods seruice, reduction of soules from sinne, & amending of il manners: because by foreflowing therof Gods honour is hindred, and manie soules doe eternally perish.
To incite the people to iust estimation of his preaching, the Prophet auoucheth that he is a messenger, not comming of himselfe but sent by God.
They began the new work the 24. day of the sixt month: and the 21. of the 7. month the Prophet had another reuelation.

Iacob the Patriarch, Gen. 49. prophecied that Christ should be the expectation of the Gentils. Who is called the desired of al Nations, because he was hertofore wanting, & alwayes necessarie to al nations.

The temple reedified after the captiuitie was not so glorious as that which Salomon built. But Christs Church of the new Testament, in which he dwelleth spiritually, farre excelleth the material temple.

That which touched a holy thing was sanctified. Leui. 6. v. 18. but the thing so touched did not sanctifie other things: so the people by touching the sacrifices were legally sanctified, but not really: & therfore their sacrifices were not grateful to God, so long as they did not endeuour to build the temple.
Al other Kingdomes perishing the Kingdom of Christ, which is his Church, is neuer destroyed.

When God in the holy Scriptures faith: Conuert to me, and I wil conuert to you, we are admonished that we haue free wil. And when we answer: Conuert vs ô Lord to thee, and we shal be conuerted, we confesse that Gods grace preuenteth vs. Conc. Trid. sess. 6. c. 5.
That this was an Angel in the shape of a man is manifest. v. 11.

Seuentie yeares from the transmigration of Ioachin were complete in the first of Cyrus. Dan. 9. Seuentie yeares were also complete from the destruction of the temple in the second of Darius Histaspis.

From foure parts of the world, to wit the Moabites & Ammonites on the east of Iurie; the Idumeans and Egyptians on the south; the Philisthiims on the west: & the Assirians and Chaldees on the Northside, had much molested the Iewes; al which were therfore plagued & punished.

According to S. Augustins rule in Psal 71. when greater things are said then can be verified as the letter foundeth, the same is literally to be vnderstood of some thing prefigured. And so this prophecie pertayneth to the Church of Christ, rather then to the citie of Ierusalem.
O ye Gentils that remaine in confused Babylon of this world, fly from it into the Church.
And ye Iewes that haue felowship with Babylon, leaue it, & serue God sincerely.
Literally this vision perteyned to the high Priest of that time.
Whose fault is here taxed, for that he admonished not the people to build the temple; and to abstaine fro marying women of strange nations, as 1.Esd. 8. 9. 10.
Angels are promised to assist the Prelates of the Church.
Christ according to his manhood is the seruant of God. Of whom S. Luke expoundeth this prophecie.
The candlesticke Metaphorically signifieth Christs Church. The lampe of light Christ. Seuen lights al the Pastours.
Two oliues Enoch & Elias. Apoc. 11.
Which vision was to be declared to Zorobabel, for his consolation that he might know, that God would protect his Church.
Two branches, the diuine & humane natures of Christ.
In this Book or rol of papers were written the sinnes of the people, and designed punishment.
It appeared flying, to signifie that this decree of punishment came from heauen. S.Chry. ho. 27. ad popul.
Execration & obduration fel vpon the Iewes, for their auarice & periurie.
Antichrist shal begin his reigne where Babylon was first built. Gen. 11.
Foure Monarchies of the Chaldees, the Medes, and Persians, the Grecians, and the Romans. Dan. 2.

When the Prophet set the crowne on the high Priests head, he explicateth that God reuealed this mysterie, saying: Behold a man, who is also God, called Orient, that is: Raising vp and establishing the Kingdom which was promised to Dauid. S.Iero.
Because the temple was burned in the fift month, & Godolias slaine in the seuenth. 4.Reg.25.v.8. 25. the Iewes fasted in those two months al the time of their captiuitie.
Which fast was good, but vnperfect, wanting works of mercie, most especially required in fastes. Isa. 58. S.Greg. And therfore the Prophet admonisheth to fast from al sinnes.
These benefts here prophecied are greater then euer were bestowed vpon the Iewes before Christ came, & therfore are rather to be vnderstood of the graces of the new testament.

The ten tribes were leaded captiue into Assiria, & the two tribes into Babylon: both which are northward not into the east nor west. And therfore this prophecie is of al nations Iewes & Gentils to be called to Christ from al parts.
Not only the fast of the fift and seuenth months but also of the fourth & tenth, were to be left off in the times of ioy and festiuitie.
Preaching true doctrine is at first vngratefull to some hearers, & stil to the incredulous, but this burden becommeth light to the faithful.
Christ came often into Ierusalem, but this last comming excelled al the rest, when he came to dye for redemption of mankind.
S. Ierom, S Cyril, & other fathers vnderstand this lake to be Limbus Patrum: from whence Christ deliuered the Saints of the old testament.
Christ is the graine of wheat which dying bringeth much fruit. Ioan. 12. And of this wheat that bread is made that came fro heauen. Ioan 6. S. Ierom in hunc locum.

Lateward time is when fruit waxeth ripe, and so is here taken for the time of grace: which S. Paul calleth the acceptable time: & therfore this Prophet exhorteth to aske this grace, and al spiritual benefts of God.
Not only the two tribes but also the ten shal be conuerted at last to Christ.
Christians are sweetly drawne by internal inspiration without clamours, and violent perswasion of words. S. Cyril.
When the faithful are multiplied & confirmed in religion, their enemies can not hurt them.

Ierusalem is called Libanus for the great beautie therof: likewise the temple. And so by this metaphore the destruction of the citie and temple by Titus is here prophecied. God the Creatour & gouernour of al men, calleth his general gouernment Beautie, and his peculiar gouernment of the Iewes a Cord.
Christ bought and sold for 30. pence.
The Iewes are reiected.
Antichrist a destroyer, shal be destroyed.
When the Church of Christ began in Ierusalem, proceeding to al Iurie, and Samaria, and to other nations, the other Iewes most earnestly persecuted Christians. Act. 4. 5. &c.
Iuda besieged Ierusalem when Iewes remaining in Iudaisme persecuted other Iewes belieuing in Christ, for then brother deliuered brother to death, the father the sonne &c. Mat. 10. v. 21.

A towne neer to Iezrahel in the countrie of Mageddon, where Iosias was slaine. 2. Paral. 15. and great lamentation was made for him which was a figure of the miserable calamitie of the obstinate in the day of iudgement. S. Ierom.
In the time of the new Testament Christ is made an open fountaine of grace by his Incarnation.
False doctrine, as idolatrie & heresie, are punishable by death in the law of Christ. By sword is vnderstood al sorts of persecution that followed vpon our Sauiour.
The Apostles flying God called them & strengthned them with fortitude.
Neither Iewes nor Gentils remaining in their proper professions can be saued, but Christian Catholikes liuing iustly, which are Gods proper people, distinct from the rest by his grace.
In the armie of the Romans were souldiers of many nations at the last destruction of Ierusalem.
Amos ch. 1. maketh also mention of this earthquake, and Iosephus. l. 9. c. 11. Antiq.
Christian doctrine of the Catholike Church vniuersal in al places and al times.
These calamities wil fal vpon the wicked neer the day of Iudgement, as Luc. 21. v. 26.
Such as before persecuted the Church shal be conuerted, & with great deuotion wil celebrate the festiuities, and exercise religious rites to Gods honour: and shal merite great rewards.
The obstinate incredulous shal remaine barren without grace, and voide of eternal glorie.
The pure oblation of Christs body and blood prophecied.

Priests coueting & scraping riches doe greatly dishonour God: diminish the estimation of holy Sacraments & other rites, as though they were temporal to be bought & sold for money, and so doe scandalize the weake.

Such are happie if God by suffering them to be spoyled, recall them to repentance. For otherwise they wil be depriued of eternal reward, as being payed their wages already in this world. These our Sauiour calleth Hyrelings, not true Pastours. Ioa. 10.
The proper office of Priests besides the administration of Sacraments is also to teach the people true doctrine, as being the Angel, that is to say, the messenger, from God.
Which holy functions Priests not performing are made contemptible in this world and miserable in eternal torments. Ep. Iud. v. 11.

S. Iohn Baptist is called an Angel or messenger, because he was to be sent with special commission from God: and for his puritie in Angelical life. S. Iohn was first conceiued, likewise first borne, and he first preached, and shortly after him our Sauiour came.
In the meane time God threatneth to punish al, euen secret sinnes, knowen to him though not to other witnesses.
Paying of tithes is most strictly commanded.
Sinne of murmuring against God, is great blasphemie & not tolerable: For when they were punished by famine, for defrauding the Leuites of tithes, they blasphemously imputed it to God.
In the day of iudgement shal plainly appeare, what difference is between the iust and the wicked.
The Septuagint for explication adde Thesbites. And S. Ierom in 17. Mat. teacheth that Elias shal indeed come and restore al things.

Christs first comming was in al meekenes not in terrour, but his coming to iudge wil be dreadfal. And therfore the Prophet here meaneth not S. Iohn Baptist, but that Elias himself shal come before the great and dreadfal day of our Lord.
